mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending economic slowdown, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real estate sales combined with expanding housing starts indicate strong consumer demand and credit availability, which jointly drive corporate revenue growth and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, elevated industrial production levels indicate capacity constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during late-cycle phases, or Rank IC turns negative when regime filter is applied.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and consumer spending power, which supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, it signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or hit rate for negative returns drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth paired with low unemployment creates an overheating economy where demand outstrips supply, leading to inflationary pressures and subsequent negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, declining federal funds rates stimulate risk appetite and lower borrowing costs, which amplifies equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power or reverses direction during easing periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 when regime filter is active.
